label: Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas

node.label: Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas
node.definition: Explicit entities, relations, types, and constraints represent structured world knowledge.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas is to connect Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data, Databases & information retrieval to downstream capabilities including Neuro-symbolic & physics-informed learning, Expert systems, production rules & rule engines, Neuro-symbolic models.
examples: substrate for Expert systems, production rules & rule engines
limitations: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data, Databases & information retrieval.

dependencies.rationale: Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas consumes, emits, or organizes information using Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data.
Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Databases & information retrieval.
Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Discrete mathematics & logic.
